retrieved_context.title: Trust boundary analysis for web UI
retrieved_context.text: A device web interface represents a trust boundary between user and system. Weak authentication or unencrypted communication reduces this boundary and increases exposure risk.

retrieved_context.title: Transport security implications
retrieved_context.text: If the web interface uses unencrypted HTTP, credentials and session tokens may be exposed to local attackers. This directly weakens trust boundaries between user and device.

retrieved_context.title: Trust boundary in local network
retrieved_context.text: Local network exposure should not be assumed safe. Devices must enforce proper authentication even within internal networks.

retrieved_context.title: Configuration exposure reasoning
retrieved_context.text: Administrative interfaces should be restricted and protected. If configuration endpoints are accessible without strong controls, this increases risk of unauthorized modification.

tutor_output.hint: Consider investigating whether the login page requires strong, unique credentials or if it might accept default or weak passwords. Also, think about whether the web interface uses encrypted communication to protect these credentials during login.
tutor_output.rationale: Weak or default authentication credentials and unencrypted HTTP traffic can significantly weaken the trust boundary between users and the device, exposing it to unauthorized access and interception risks.
tutor_output.check_question: What evidence can you gather to determine if the login mechanism enforces strong authentication and if the communication channel is encrypted or not?

assistant_output.suggestion: Next, examine whether the login page accepts weak or default credentials and verify if authentication enforcement is consistent.
assistant_output.explanation: Weak or default credentials are a common insecure configuration that directly weakens the trust boundary between the user and device. Confirming this vulnerability helps identify critical security gaps in the web interface.
assistant_output.safety_note: Perform all tests within the authorised lab environment using low-impact methods to avoid disrupting device operation or network stability.
